title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for versioned migrations, expand-contract changes, and rollback evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for rehearsed schema and data migration chain. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.
Decide transaction boundaries from each PostgreSQL operation and separate concurrent index, long data, and lock-sensitive work.
Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Migration Ledger · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 2857 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Migration Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Migration Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 2582 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test fresh install, upgrade, interrupted backfill, retry, rollback or forward repair, backup restore, and version skew.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Migration Recovery.
